summary: This brief blog post serves as a link roundup sharing two of Cathy O'Neil's Bloomberg opinion columns. The first column argues that pandemic-driven automation could present an opportunity for workers to negotiate better terms with their 'robot overlords.' The second is a personal reflection mourning the impact of COVID-19 on New York City's subway system. The post itself contains no substantive analysis, functioning purely as a pointer to her longer-form work elsewhere.
key_points: The pandemic is accelerating workplace automation trends
O'Neil sees potential for workers to negotiate amid automation disruption
COVID-19 is fundamentally changing urban infrastructure like public transit
The NYC subway represents something worth mourning as a civic institution
money_quotes: The pandemic will accelerate automation. This might be an opportunity for workers.
The pandemic is dealing one of the city's greatest features a brutal blow.
key_insight: Major disruptions like pandemics accelerate automation but may also create windows for workers to reshape the terms of that transition.
